title: Engineer in Society
outline: This course frames engineering as a socio-technical endeavour and deals with the broader context in which engineering activity takes place. This includes the economic systems in which engineering organisations are embedded; the socio -economic status of com munities that might feel the impact from engineering activity; explores, through site visits, the notion of ‘community’ in post -apartheid South Africa; and deals with the political systems in which engineering operations occur, including issues of labour relations.
dp_requirements: Please refer to the official course handout document for detailed information regarding the DP requirements for this course.
assessment: Please refer to the official course handout document regarding the assessment criteria for this course.
